scenario: You see a woman spanking her child with a spatula for getting bad grades in school.
moral_expl: The child is vulnerable, the action is intentional, and harm is caused, making it morally unacceptable.

scenario: I was supposed to go see my father before he died. He was ill and my mother was supposed to come into town so we could see him. She didn't come, so we never made it before he passed away.
moral_expl: Mother's intentional absence caused harm, vulnerability, and missed opportunity for last goodbye.

scenario: Person X stepped in when a friend at a pub was getting assaulted for being dark skinned.
moral_expl: Intentional action to prevent harm, helping a vulnerable person, and causing no harm.

scenario: Gave a beggar a five-dollar bill at Christmas time
moral_expl: The action shows kindness and generosity towards a vulnerable individual, causing no harm.

scenario: You are the safety officer in charge of a fun park. One of the metal arms of the ferris wheel suddenly breaks because of a structural defect. Four people are stranded in a cabin 80 metres up in the air. Another person is in a cabin just a few metres from ground level and is able to get off alone. The whole structure is falling down.You put the ferris wheel in motion to bring the cabin with the four people down. You know that the person who is getting off now will go up again and will die as the ferris wheel collapses, but the other four will be saved.
moral_expl: The action saves four lives, but intentionally sacrifices one, weighing moral acceptability.

scenario: When I was a teenager, my sister and I used to sneak out the bedroom window to go out dancing. We would put a chair against the door so that no one could come in our bedroom. A couple of times we got caught.
moral_expl: Teenagers' sneaking out was intentional, caused some harm, but no severe consequences.

scenario: Person X ordered pizza while at their friend's farewell party.
moral_expl: Ordering pizza at a friend's farewell party is a neutral, harmless act.

scenario: It is evening. A friend calls you saying that he has a terrible migraine headache. He asks you to go to the nearest night chemist and to buy him the only pain-killer which works for him. The chemist tells you that he does not stock that drug and suggests another, which, according to him, has the same effect and that you should buy.You buy the migraine drug that the chemist suggests. You make your friend take it without showing him the box. He will think he is taking the one he asked for.
moral_expl: Deception and lack of informed consent outweigh potential benefit, causing moral ambiguity.

scenario: Took a friend out for a beer to celebrate his promotion
moral_expl: Celebrating a friend's promotion with a beer is a harmless and supportive act.

scenario: Fires teargas into a crowd of civilians.
moral_expl: Fires teargas into a crowd of civilians, causing harm and violating human rights.

scenario: Complimented coworkers on their clothing and appearance
moral_expl: Compliments can boost morale, no harm intended or caused, and may foster positive work relationships.

scenario: I was in a relationship with my best friend's cousin. She was really beautiful, but as time went on I became less attracted to her. I tried to tell her I didn't really want to see her anymore and she flipped out.
moral_expl: The scenario is morally neutral, as the person's feelings changed, but they handled the situation poorly.

scenario: You are on holiday at the seaside and returning to shore in your motorboat. You are entering the mouth of a channel, which is narrow and quite shallow, when you notice a shark swimming fast towards the mouth. At the end of the channel is a swimmer, who is calmly bathing. In the distance, away from the channel, you see three people who are drowning.You turn quickly out of the channel, leaving the mouth free. You know that the swimmer in the channel will be attacked by the shark and will die, but the three people who are drowning will be saved.
moral_expl: The action prioritizes saving three lives over one, with minimal intention to harm the swimmer.

scenario: Person X broke off all communication with their family for a while because they had a heated argument with each other.
moral_expl: Person X's action, though hurtful, was likely an emotional response to a heated argument.

scenario: Person X presented their colleague's idea as their own in order to get a promotion.
moral_expl: Intentional deception causing harm to colleague, no help caused, and unfair advantage taken.

scenario: You see an employee joking with competitors about how bad his company did last year.
moral_expl: Employee's joking about company's poor performance with competitors shows disloyalty and lack of professionalism.

scenario: Someone posts on social media bragging about making out with multiple strangers the night before
moral_expl: Lack of respect for others' boundaries and potential harm to others' physical and emotional well-being.

scenario: You see a girl shooting geese repeatedly with a pellet gun out in the woods.
moral_expl: Intentional harm to vulnerable animals with no apparent benefit or justification.

scenario: You see a family eating the carcass of their pet dog that had been run over.
moral_expl: While the action is disturbing, it's a desperate act in a survival situation with no harm intended.

scenario: Person X stole the collection tin of a blind beggar on the street.
moral_expl: Exploiting a vulnerable individual, intentional harm, and no help caused makes it extremely immoral.

scenario: Refused to hold the door for a man in a wheelchair
moral_expl: Refusing to hold the door for a person in need, especially with a disability, shows a lack of empathy.

scenario: Spent time voluntarily tutoring disadvantaged students
moral_expl: The tutor's intentional action caused significant help to vulnerable students, making it highly moral.

scenario: Person X saw a homeless person in the rain, so they gave the person their jacket and umbrella, plus $20 for a hot meal
moral_expl: The action shows empathy and kindness towards a vulnerable person, causing no harm and providing significant help.

scenario: For a while I was a security officer at a bank. One day I was walking by the front door when I saw a fifty-dollar bill. I was tempted to take the money but instead I reported it to the manager.
moral_expl: The security officer resisted temptation and reported the found money, demonstrating honesty and integrity.

scenario: Heckled a woman speaking on human rights
moral_expl: Heckling a woman speaking on human rights is a deliberate act of disrespect and intimidation, causing harm and silencing a vital voice.

scenario: Criticized an old woman for being too slow
moral_expl: Criticizing an elderly person for being slow is hurtful and shows lack of empathy.

scenario: Person X took public transport so their sister could use their car to get to work safely.
moral_expl: Selfless act to ensure sister's safety, no harm caused, and help provided.

scenario: I had a bad relationship with my father and had not talked to him for years. He had left my mother. When my mother died I gathered the strength to call him and tell him that she died and that I loved him.
moral_expl: The scenario shows forgiveness, empathy, and courage in a vulnerable situation.

scenario: My husband ran a business and I worked in his shop. Once a rather unsavory character came in and handed my husband something. So, I looked through my husband's wallet to see what the man gave him.
moral_expl: Privacy violation, but motivated by concern for husband's safety and potential harm.

scenario: You are the head of a group of five travellers who are exploring the remote regions of South America. During the trip, you inadvertently camp in the sacred cemetery of a local tribe. The members of the tribe capture you and condemn you all to death. After some negotiation, they propose a possible alternative in the form of a human sacrifice that will pacify their god who has been offended.You accept the tribe’s proposal, and during the ceremony, you kill one of the travellers in the group. This person will die, but you and the other four travellers’ lives will be spared.
moral_expl: Killing an innocent person to save others, despite being under duress, is still morally reprehensible.

scenario: Rescued an injured kitten from a tree
moral_expl: Helping a vulnerable kitten in distress with intentional action, causing no harm and providing significant help.

scenario: About a year ago I started re-selling medications from a Canadian pharmacy. I helped a great deal of elderly people so that they didn't have to pay exorbitant amounts for their medications that they needed.
moral_expl: Helping vulnerable elderly people access necessary medications, but illegal and potentially harmful.

scenario: I was at the pharmacy buying something and I noticed a man who was sitting outside selling trinkets. He was homeless and it was freezing out. So I went next door to a store and bought him some food and new clothes.
moral_expl: The scenario shows intentional kindness towards a vulnerable person, causing significant help and no harm.

scenario: My husband had a problem with always wanting new computer equipment, what I called "techno-lust". It was a really huge financial problem, and I told him he had to go on a 5-year plan to financially police himself or I would divorce him. He has greatly improved.
moral_expl: The wife's ultimatum was a necessary measure to address a significant financial problem.

scenario: Took the neighbor's newspaper
moral_expl: Minor harm, intentional action, but no vulnerable party involved.

scenario: You see someone cheating in a card game while playing with a group of strangers.
moral_expl: Cheating in a card game with strangers is intentional and causes harm to others' trust and fairness.

scenario: Person X did really well at the quiz night.
moral_expl: No harm or vulnerability involved, only achievement and possible joy.

scenario: Sang at a prison in order to entertain the inmates
moral_expl: Singing at a prison brings joy to inmates, a positive and harmless act.

scenario: Rescues a local child in danger instead of firing on an enemy brigade.
moral_expl: The action prioritizes saving an innocent child's life over military objectives, demonstrating compassion and humanity.

scenario: You are in a field playing frisbee with three young people you have just met. Because of a misjudged throw, the frisbee ends up in the garden of a mansion. You decide to climb the wall to look for it. Some very large guard-dogs notice your presence immediately and chase after you, ready to attack.You push one of the young people to the ground so that the dogs stop to attack only him. You know that he will die, but you, and the other two, will be able to escape.
moral_expl: The action is extremely immoral as it intentionally sacrifices an innocent person to save oneself.

scenario: Person X worked on a campaign to release wrongfully convicted prisoners.
moral_expl: Helping wrongfully convicted prisoners is a highly moral act, promoting justice and fairness.

scenario: Gave his coat to someone when it was cold
moral_expl: The person showed kindness and empathy by giving their coat to someone in need.

scenario: Kicked a stray cat to get it to leave his yard
moral_expl: The cat was vulnerable, intentional harm was caused, and no help was provided.

scenario: Tricked a stay at home mom into donating to a fake charity
moral_expl: Exploiting a vulnerable individual for personal gain is highly immoral and causes harm.

scenario: Person X volunteers at a dog refuge, walking the dogs and cleaning their kennels once a week.
moral_expl: Volunteering at a dog refuge shows compassion and kindness, causing no harm and providing help.

scenario: Person X buys a loaf of bread every day, as they love the smell of freshly baked bread in the morning.
moral_expl: No harm or vulnerability, only personal preference and potential environmental impact.

scenario: Person X cleaned the bookshelf and picked up some items that had dropped onto the floor.
moral_expl: The action is neutral, no harm or help was caused, and intention was to clean.

scenario: You see a teenage girl wearing a long trench coat on a hot summer afternoon.
moral_expl: The girl's choice may be unusual, but it doesn't harm others or herself.

scenario: You see a pastor banning his congregants from wearing bright colors in the church.
moral_expl: Restricting personal expression without a valid reason can be seen as oppressive and controlling.

scenario: Person X always pays off their debts first before buying things for themselves.
moral_expl: Responsible financial behavior, prioritizing obligations over personal desires.

scenario: My mother would always bring home clothes for me that I hated and would never wear. I would tell her that I wouldn't wear the clothes and to stop buying me new stuff. She would get really mad at me.
moral_expl: Mother's actions were unintentionally harmful, but child's autonomy was disrespected.

scenario: Lent money to a friend in a financial crisis
moral_expl: Helping a friend in need with no intention to harm, minimal harm, and significant help.

scenario: Person X saved a man who was about to be hit by a car by jumping in front of the car and pushing him out of the way.
moral_expl: Selfless act to save a life with no harm caused to others.

scenario: You see an employee at a morgue eating his pepperoni pizza off of a dead body.
moral_expl: The action is extremely disrespectful and unprofessional, causing harm to the deceased's dignity.

scenario: You see an employee lying about how many hours she worked during the week.
moral_expl: Intentional deception causes harm to employer and undermines trust, with no help caused.

scenario: Pushed into the front of a line at a theater
moral_expl: Intentional pushing, causing inconvenience and potential harm, with no help provided.

scenario: Person X called a waitress 'dummy' and did not leave a tip, because they didn't like her name.
moral_expl: Intentional harm and disrespect towards a vulnerable service worker with no justification.

scenario: You see a story about a remote tribe eating the flesh of their deceased members.
moral_expl: Cultural context and potential nutritional necessity, but still a taboo and harmful practice.

scenario: Someone brags on social media about drinking excessively to the point of vomiting
moral_expl: The scenario promotes harmful behavior, puts oneself and others at risk, and shows a lack of self-awareness.

scenario: Didn't leave a tip at the restaurant
moral_expl: Intentional action, moderate harm to vulnerable worker, no help caused.

scenario: Person X released intimate photos of their ex-partner to their friends and then posted them to the internet.
moral_expl: The scenario is extremely immoral due to intentional violation of privacy and trust, causing significant harm.

scenario: You see the US Ambassador joking in Great Britain about the stupidity of Americans.
moral_expl: Ambassador's joke about Americans' stupidity is disrespectful and harmful, causing offense and damaging reputation.

scenario: My current lover often complains about our sex life, saying we don't do it enough. Sometimes I will watch online porn and masturbate instead. Sometimes, I feel like it's just easier and more fun to do that.
moral_expl: The scenario shows a lack of effort to address relationship issues and may lead to emotional harm.

scenario: Had a welcoming party for some new neighbors
moral_expl: The scenario shows kindness and hospitality, causing no harm and providing a sense of community.

scenario: You are the commander of a Russian military submarine on a mission. You have been trapped for days at the bottom of the Pacific Ocean without energy. You have already asked for help several times, but there are no ships or submarines in the nearby waters and help is taking time to arrive. There are six crew members with you on board and your food supplies are getting low.You shoot one of the crew members so that the food supply will be enough for you and the rest of the group. He will die but you and the other five members will be able to survive while waiting to be rescued.
moral_expl: The commander's intentional harm to a vulnerable crew member outweighs the potential benefit to others.

scenario: You see a boy turning up the TV as his father talks about his military service.
moral_expl: The boy's action is disrespectful and inconsiderate, causing harm to his father's emotional well-being.

scenario: I was working at a fast food restaurant and the policy was to throw away the excess food at the end of the night. Even if there was a large quantity of food left over you had to throw it away. Instead my friend and I would take home the extra food.
moral_expl: Reducing food waste, helping individuals, but disobeying company policy.

scenario: Person X left their long-term band as soon as an opportunity came up to play with a more successful band.
moral_expl: Person X's intentional abandonment caused harm to their original band, showing disloyalty and selfishness.

scenario: You are in charge of an expedition which is crossing the Sahara. A sand storm causes your jeep to swerve off the road and overturn in the middle of the desert. You know that the nearest oasis is a week’s walk away. There are eight of you and a very limited amount of water, which will not allow you all to survive the scorching heat.You shoot two people so that there will be enough water for you and the rest of the group. These two will die, but you and the other five will be able to survive.
moral_expl: The action is intentional and causes harm, but also saves lives in a desperate situation.

scenario: Person X was able to convince their boss that they were ready for a promotion at work.
moral_expl: No harm caused, person's initiative and confidence led to a positive outcome.

scenario: You see a woman getting hired only because her father is close friends with the boss.
moral_expl: Nepotism leads to unfair advantage, undermining merit-based hiring and causing harm to others.

scenario: Stole into a parking place while someone else was backing in
moral_expl: Intentional and inconsiderate action causing frustration and potential harm to another driver.

scenario: A few years ago, I was single and I went to see a friend of mine in the Bahamas. I was extremely attracted to a man I met there who was married. I danced as close to the fire as I could, but I did nothing.
moral_expl: Resisted temptation, no harm caused, and no intentional wrongdoing.

scenario: As I was backing out of a parking lot I bumped a parked car and left a minor dent. I didn't even feel the impact when I hit the car but it left a little bit of damage. I drove away without leaving a message or trying to contact the person.
moral_expl: The driver's intentional lack of responsibility and harm caused to another's property makes it immoral.

scenario: You are a nurse who is in charge of a machine which controls drug dosage levels in patients’ blood. Because of a technical failure, the machine is supplying a lethal dose of the drug to four patients. Another patient, in a single room, is hooked up to the same machine and has not undergone any variation in dosage.Press the button to block the drug supply to the four patients. You know that the overdose of drug will be redirected to the patient in the single room, who will die, but the other four will be saved.
moral_expl: The action is morally acceptable as it saves four lives, despite causing one death.

scenario: Person X went to a fancy restaurant but couldn't pronounce the items on the menu.
moral_expl: No harm or intention to harm, just a personal struggle with pronunciation.

scenario: Recently I was supposed to go to a meeting with a whole bunch of people who are not my friends, just acquaintances. I really didn't want to go. So I emailed the group saying that I had to work that day, when in reality I didn't.
moral_expl: Deception and dishonesty, but minimal harm and no vulnerable party involved.

scenario: You are in the head office of your bank together with four other people. Suddenly, the director calls you because he has discovered a bomb in an office on the ground floor. He knows you are a bomb disposal expert and asks you to defuse it. You realise immediately that there is not enough time to evacuate the people in the bank before the bomb explodes.You throw the bomb into the basement where there is the security vault. You know that the explosion will kill the security guard in the vault, but you and the other four people will be saved.
moral_expl: The action, though intentional, saves four lives at the cost of one, making it a difficult moral choice.

scenario: You are driving your new and very expensive car along a narrow country road. On the edge of the road you see a person who has just fallen off their bicycle. This person has hit their head violently and asks you if you can accompany them to the nearest hospital. You realise that because of the fall the person is now covered with mud.You leave the injured person on the side of the road. You know that sooner or later somebody will pass by and, in this way, you will not dirty the leather seats in your new car.
moral_expl: Leaving a vulnerable, injured person in need of help to protect a material possession is extremely immoral.

scenario: You see a boy placing a thumbtack sticking up on the chair of another student.
moral_expl: Intentional harm, vulnerable victim, potential physical harm, no help caused.

scenario: You see a man telling his girlfriend that she must convert to his religion.
moral_expl: Coercion and manipulation in a personal relationship, potentially causing emotional distress and undermining autonomy.

scenario: Ate all the food before anyone else could have any
moral_expl: Intentional action causing harm to others, no help or benefit to anyone.

scenario: You see a man staying inside his home with the shades drawn on a rare sunny day.
moral_expl: The man's action seems harmless and personal, not affecting others negatively.

scenario: Accidentally wounds a young soldier when firing a warning shot.
moral_expl: Unintentional harm to a vulnerable individual, but with a warning shot intention.

scenario: Left food out for stray dogs
moral_expl: Helping vulnerable stray dogs with food, minimal harm, and good intentions.

scenario: Person X scratched their neighbor's expensive car with a key, as he always parked it at the front of PersonX's house.
moral_expl: Intentional harm to property, no help caused, and significant harm inflicted.

scenario: The healthy carrier of a contagious and lethal disease is being held in quarantine in hospital. Suddenly the ventilation system breaks down and there is no longer a change of air in the room. The emergency system will shortly be activated and an internal window will be opened. This window opens into a ward in which five patients are being treated for various illnesses.You block the emergency system by pressing a button which will keep the window closed. You know that the healthy carrier will suffocate, but the five patients will be saved from mortal contagion.
moral_expl: The action prioritizes the lives of five patients over one, minimizing harm and maximizing help.

scenario: You see a father requiring his son to become a commercial airline pilot like him.
moral_expl: Forcing a career choice on someone can lead to unhappiness and resentment.

scenario: Someone yells at a server at a restaurant because their meal is late
moral_expl: Yelling at a server is unacceptable, causing emotional harm and distress.

scenario: I said some bad things about this guy that I work with. He told me some secrets that I promised not to share, but I did anyway. He didn't find out but I was afraid if he did, he might do something crazy.
moral_expl: Breaking trust and confidentiality causes harm, even if not discovered.

scenario: Person X punched a woman for wearing a hijab because PersonX thinks they should not be allowed in this country.
moral_expl: The scenario involves a violent and discriminatory act against a vulnerable individual, causing harm and no help.

scenario: When I was a football coach, I was often involved with the moms of the kids on the team. There were many mothers that were attractive and made themselves available. I was very tempted to have sex with one mother who came on to me.
moral_expl: Power imbalance, potential harm to relationships and reputation, and exploitation of trust.

scenario: Executes a squad of surrendering enemy troops.
moral_expl: Executing surrendering troops is a war crime, violating international humanitarian law and basic human rights.

scenario: Refuses to fire into a crowd to stop an enemy combatant.
moral_expl: Refusing to fire into a crowd prioritizes civilian safety over military objectives.

scenario: Pulled his dog's tail
moral_expl: Intentional harm to a vulnerable animal, causing unnecessary pain and distress.

scenario: Beat up someone smaller than him
moral_expl: Intentional harm caused to a vulnerable individual with no help provided.

scenario: Refused to loan class notes to a friend who had been ill
moral_expl: Refusing to help a vulnerable friend in need, causing harm and no help.

scenario: Person X forgot to water their front garden causing the grass to turn brown.
moral_expl: Negligence, but no harm to humans or animals, only aesthetic damage.

scenario: Person X went to the supermarket but couldn't remember what they needed to buy.
moral_expl: No harm or intention to harm, just a forgetful moment.

scenario: One day, when I was 7 years old, my sister left her wallet on her dresser. I bumped the dresser and the wallet fell onto the floor. Many coins came out and I picked them up and put them in my pocket.
moral_expl: Child's impulsive action without malicious intent, minimal harm, and no help caused.

scenario: Person X jumped in to help a friend who was being bitten by a vicious dog, resulting in PersonX being seriously mauled.
moral_expl: Person X's selfless act to save a friend from harm outweighs the harm caused to themselves.

scenario: Broke a friend's phone and neither apologized or replaced it
moral_expl: Intentional harm, no apology or restitution, causing significant distress and financial loss.

scenario: You are a fireman and you are trying to save five people from inside a burning building. The only window from which the people can be evacuated is jammed and will not open. The fire will reach you in a short time. Outside on the window ledge of the floor below, there is a person who is waiting to be saved.With an axe you smash the window to get out. You know that when it falls, the heavy glass will kill the person on the lower floor, but you and the five people in danger will be able to escape.
moral_expl: The action is morally acceptable due to the greater good and minimal alternative.

scenario: Laughed at a friend's joke even though it wasn't funny
moral_expl: Laughing at a friend's joke to avoid hurting their feelings is a kind and considerate act.

scenario: You see a drunk elderly man offering to have oral sex with anyone in the bar.
moral_expl: The scenario is morally unacceptable due to the vulnerable state of the elderly man and potential harm to others.

scenario: Person X offered to pay off the debts of their friend, who had been struggling to pay the bills since their partner died.
moral_expl: The action is highly moral as it shows empathy and kindness towards a vulnerable friend in need.

scenario: Gave out toys to the Children's Hospital at Christmas
moral_expl: Vulnerable patients benefited from intentional, harmless, and helpful actions.

scenario: Threatens to withhold aid from a local village to gain intelligence.
moral_expl: Coercion and exploitation of vulnerable people for personal gain is highly immoral.

scenario: Lied to his girlfriend about not having other dates
moral_expl: Intentional deception causes harm to trust and relationship, with no apparent help or justification.

scenario: Worked all summer to give his parents a trip to Hawaii
moral_expl: Selfless act of kindness, no harm caused, and significant help provided to loved ones.

scenario: You see a girl taking all the Halloween candy from a bowl, leaving none for others.
moral_expl: Intentional and harmful action, no help caused, and vulnerable others affected.

scenario: Person X ate their lunch and went back to work with food stuck in their teeth.
moral_expl: Lack of intentionality and minimal harm, but potential embarrassment and social discomfort.

scenario: You see a woman commenting out loud about how fat another woman looks in her jeans.
moral_expl: Publicly shaming someone for their appearance is harmful and intentional, causing emotional distress.

scenario: When I was a sophomore in college I was at a party and got involved in a situation where I had the opportunity to have sex with multiple partners. I had sex with all three of them in the same night. There were no emotional or physical repercussions after.
moral_expl: Consensual, no harm or help, intentional but vulnerable due to party setting.

scenario: I used to work as a legal assistant. I lied to clients all of the time, the attorney I worked for lied all the time too. We just lie to our clients so things will work out the best for us.
moral_expl: Intentional deception of vulnerable clients causes harm and undermines trust in the legal profession.

scenario: Got into a fight in a bar with an acquaintance
moral_expl: Fighting in a bar is reckless and harmful, causing physical and emotional harm to others.

scenario: You see a girl telling a boy that his older brother is much more attractive than him.
moral_expl: The girl's intentional comment causes harm to the boy's self-esteem with no apparent benefit.

scenario: Failed to return a borrowed book for months
moral_expl: Borrower's negligence causes inconvenience, but no severe harm or malicious intent.

scenario: Paid for a friend's dinner
moral_expl: Act of kindness, no harm caused, and help provided to a friend in need.

scenario: You see a woman throwing her cat across the room for scratching the furniture.
moral_expl: The woman's intentional and harmful action towards a vulnerable animal is extremely immoral.

scenario: I was playing basketball near a beach. I was hanging out all day when I noticed that there was a basketball that no one had claimed. There was no one around so I took the basketball.
moral_expl: Taking an unclaimed basketball with no harm or help caused is a neutral, slightly opportunistic act.

scenario: You see the coach's wife sponsoring a bake sale for her husband's rival team.
moral_expl: The coach's wife's action is unexpected, but harmless and potentially beneficial to the rival team.

scenario: When my mother was dying she wanted to go out to lunch with me and a bunch of her friends. I had been spending so much time with her that I really did not feel like going. So I told her that I needed some space for myself.
moral_expl: The action was unintentionally hurtful, but understandable given caregiver burnout.

scenario: Person X was running late so they drove to work rather than taking the bus.
moral_expl: No harm or vulnerability involved, minor inconvenience to others, and convenience to self.

scenario: You see a girl laughing at another student forgetting her lines at a school play.
moral_expl: Laughter at someone's mistake can be hurtful, but no intentional harm was meant.

scenario: Scratched a stranger's car with his key
moral_expl: Intentional harm to stranger's property with no benefit or justification.

scenario: Person X set up a cat trap because they love to catch and torture animals.
moral_expl: Intentional harm and torture of vulnerable animals is extremely immoral and inhumane.

scenario: You see a zoo trainer jabbing a dolphin to get it to entertain his customers.
moral_expl: Intentional harm to a vulnerable animal for human entertainment is unacceptable.

scenario: You have been invited to the birthday party of someone you do not know well. You are not really very keen on going, or on spending a lot of money on a present, because he/she is not a close friend. You find a brand-name jumper in excellent condition in a second-hand shop and buy it. On opening the present, the receiver seems embarrassed by such a generous big present.You smile satisfied and reassure the receiver, saying that an important occasion like his/her birthday deserves a special present. You know that he/she will then think of you as a generous person.
moral_expl: The scenario is morally questionable due to insincere motives and potential manipulation.

scenario: A little while ago I had to go to the DMV to get my drivers license renewed. They told me that I had to take a written test in which I had three attempts to pass. I failed the first two times so I cheated on the final attempt.
moral_expl: Cheating is intentional and unfair, causing harm to the integrity of the system.

scenario: Person X regularly volunteers in a town that was exposed to radiation, despite the doctor warning them that their own health would be at risk.
moral_expl: Person X prioritizes others' well-being over their own health, showing altruism and selflessness.

scenario: Someone tells a dirty joke at a holocaust memorial
moral_expl: Disrespectful and insensitive behavior in a sacred place causes emotional harm to those present.

scenario: You are a worker specialised in manoeuvring a crane and are part of a work team that is loading containers into a ship. You have just lifted a container from the wharf when you realise that the cable of the crane is breaking and that the container is about to crash down on you and the other workers in the team.You move the arm of the crane away from you. You know that the container will fall in an area in which there are two workers who will die crushed, but you and the other will be saved.
moral_expl: Acting to save own life and others, while unintentionally causing harm to two workers.

scenario: You see someone driving around in a dirty car that has not been washed recently.
moral_expl: Lack of car washing doesn't harm others, and intentionality is unclear.

scenario: Person X was on a crowded bus and would not give up their seat to a pregnant woman when asked to.

scenario: You see an intern disobeying an order to dress professionally and comb his hair.

scenario: You are a specialist in underwater welding. You are working with your team of three other people in a small dam at a depth of 30m under water. Because of a problem with the flow regulator, about 100 cubic metres too much water has flowed into the dam. The pressure is too great and you are unable to surface.You activate the opening of the sluice gates. The water will flow into the valley where there is an observatory. You know that a researcher works there, and he/she will be swept away in the overflow water, but you and the other three will be saved.
moral_expl: The action saves four lives but causes harm to an innocent researcher, making it morally ambiguous.

scenario: Made an obscene gesture at an old lady
moral_expl: Intentional disrespect towards a vulnerable elderly person causing emotional harm.

scenario: Person X taught their nephew how to drive at the local shopping center car park, after the shops had shut.
moral_expl: Teaching nephew to drive in an empty car park shows care and responsibility.

scenario: My sister is five years older than I am. When we were little she would always tease me about things. So one day when she was teasing me about something, I threw a pencil at her.
moral_expl: Sibling rivalry, low harm, and minor intentionality make it a relatively minor moral infraction.

scenario: Person X learned how to play the piano when they were a child.
moral_expl: Learning piano as a child is a neutral, harmless activity.

scenario: One night I was having sex with my boyfriend. He said that he had a condom on but at the end I found out that he didn't. I became pregnant and since I just had had a baby recently, I decided to have an abortion.
moral_expl: The boyfriend's deception and lack of consent led to harm, but the abortion was a difficult decision made in a vulnerable situation.

scenario: You see a Hollywood star agreeing with a foreign dictator's denunciation of the US.
moral_expl: The Hollywood star's agreement with the dictator's denunciation of the US can be seen as harmful and irresponsible.

scenario: Person X told the children to be quiet in the library.
moral_expl: The action was intentional, but minimal harm was caused and it maintained a respectful environment.

scenario: Spent time one summer teaching his daughter to play baseball
moral_expl: Teaching a child a new skill is a positive, loving act with no harm intended or caused.

scenario: Person X started a false rumor that their office colleague Mary used to be a sex worker.
moral_expl: Intentional spreading of false and harmful rumor about a vulnerable person, causing significant harm.

scenario: Gave food to the needy
moral_expl: Helping the needy is a selfless act that causes no harm and provides significant benefit.

scenario: You are at Luna Park and you have decided to take a ride on the roller coaster. You get into the carriage together with four other people. After a couple of circuits, the speed starts to increase dramatically right at the point that the carriage does a loop the loop. The technician in charge tells you over the loudspeaker that the mechanism which controls the brakes is not responding.You pull the emergency handle in the carriage that will make it divert on to another track. You can see there is a man working there. The carriage will run into him and he will die, but you and the other four will be unhurt.
moral_expl: The action is morally acceptable as it saves five lives, but causes one death.

scenario: You see a boss pressuring employees to buy goods from her family's general store.
moral_expl: Boss's pressure exploits employees' vulnerability, causing harm and no help.

scenario: Demanded a refund at a restaurant for slow service
moral_expl: Customer has right to fair service, refund justified if service was slow.

scenario: You see a woman answering a phone call with the word "goodbye" instead of "hello.
moral_expl: Unconventional greeting, but no harm or vulnerability involved.

scenario: You see a girl throwing her hot coffee on a woman who is dating her ex-boyfriend
moral_expl: Intentional harm caused to an unsuspecting person, no help provided, and vulnerability exploited.

scenario: You see someone leaving his dog outside in the rain after it dug in the trash.
moral_expl: Leaving a dog outside in the rain as punishment is cruel and neglectful.

scenario: Visited a nursing home to cheer up the patients
moral_expl: Visiting patients intentionally brings joy and comfort, causing no harm.

scenario: You see a staff member talking loudly and interrupting the mayor's speech to the public.
moral_expl: Disrupting a public speech is disrespectful and may cause disturbance, but no physical harm is done.

scenario: You are in charge of the Maritime Police. You have received a message that the captain and a seaman of a cargo ship are healthy carriers of a lethal and contagious virus. They do not know that they are infected, and are travelling towards a small gulf where fishermen live. The communications system of the cargo ship is not working and, therefore, they cannot be contacted.You launch a missile and sink the cargo ship to avoid it mooring and thus spreading the virus. You know that the captain and seaman will die, but the fishermen will be saved.
moral_expl: Tragic necessity to save many lives outweighs harm to two unknowing carriers.

scenario: You see a girl saying that another girl is too ugly to be a varsity cheerleader.
moral_expl: The girl's intentional and hurtful comment causes emotional harm to the targeted individual.

scenario: About ten years ago, I had a friend who was a musician. She was struggling and wasn't making any money. We had a nice time together and I enjoyed her company. At that time, I was making money, so I decided to give her some money.
moral_expl: The action was intentional, helpful, and caused no harm, showing kindness and generosity.

scenario: I was dating this guy who was having a nervous breakdown. His mother asked me if I would move in with him, cook for him and make sure he was okay. So I moved in with him for a while.
moral_expl: Helping a vulnerable person in need, but potential exploitation and unequal power dynamics.

scenario: Told a colleague in public that she should lose weight
moral_expl: Publicly shaming someone about their weight is harmful and disrespectful, causing emotional distress.

scenario: Gave money to charity
moral_expl: Charitable act with no harm, intentional kindness, and potential help to those in need.

scenario: You see a manager coercing her employees into eating at her brother's diner.
moral_expl: Manager's coercion exploits employees' vulnerability, causing harm and no help.

scenario: Person X is learning French as they always wanted to learn another language.
moral_expl: Learning a language is a personal choice, harmless, and beneficial.

scenario: Someone states that biological diferences between men and women make men better suited to do certain types of work and women better suited to do other types of work
moral_expl: The scenario perpetuates harmful gender stereotypes, limiting opportunities and reinforcing discrimination.

scenario: Years ago, I shoplifted lipstick from a drugstore. No matter what I did with the lipstick after I stole it, I just wasn’t able to enjoy it. I never took it back though or told anyone.
moral_expl: Intentional theft with no harm mitigation, causing financial loss to the drugstore.

scenario: My father is manic-depressive. He was very difficult to deal with and often made life really hard and everyday situations unpleasant. I talked about him in a really negative way because it was so hard for me to deal with.
moral_expl: The speaker's negative talk about their father was a coping mechanism, not intended to harm others.

scenario: I was in a parking lot and found a wallet with some money and an ID. I was near a market so I went down to the market to see if I recognized the person on the ID. I found the woman and gave her back her wallet.
moral_expl: The scenario shows high moral character, as the finder intentionally returns the wallet, causing no harm and providing significant help.

scenario: At a restaurant, when the waitress returned with my change, I was positive she was $10 short. She disagreed but gave me the $10. I told her that if the register was $10 short tonight I'd give it back to her. When I came back the next day, she said I was wrong so gave her the $10.
moral_expl: The customer's initial accusation was questionable, but their subsequent honesty redeems the situation.

scenario: You are having dinner with some friends in a restaurant in the centre of town. Suddenly, a criminal armed with a machine gun enters. He threatens to shoot at a table where a family of six is seated if his request for an outrageous amount of money is not met. You understand that the gunman has mental problems and any attempts to mediate would be in vain.You push a man who is near your table on to the gunman so that he will feel threatened and shoot him. You know that this man will die, but the six people will have time to escape.
moral_expl: The action, although intentional, saves six lives at the cost of one, in a desperate situation.

scenario: Person X played chess with their friend, winning the game.
moral_expl: Winning a game of chess is a normal and harmless activity between friends.

scenario: Person X always wins at Trivial Pursuit.
moral_expl: No harm or vulnerability involved, just a person consistently winning at a game.

scenario: Threw an acquaintance out of his home for disagreeing with him
moral_expl: The action was intentional, caused harm, and showed disrespect for others' opinions.

scenario: Someone makes a post on Facebook about how worthless teachers are
moral_expl: The post is harmful and intentionally targets a vulnerable profession, causing harm to teachers' self-esteem and morale.

scenario: You are a fireman who is trying to save some people trapped in a fire which has broken out on the 20th floor of a skyscraper, which is luckily deserted. Six people are trapped in a room on the 21st floor and are already beginning to suffocate from the smoke, while one person is coming down alone from the 19th floor on foot.Activate the automatic closure of the fire doors. You know that this will mean that the person coming down will be unable to escape from the building. He will die, but it will give you time to save the other six.
moral_expl: Tragic choice between saving six lives and sacrificing one, with intentional harm to minimize overall harm.

scenario: Worked in a state park for a summer
moral_expl: Working in a state park for a summer is a neutral, harmless activity.

scenario: Person X broke a friend's expensive vase and refused to pay to replace it.
moral_expl: Intentional harm, refusal to take responsibility, and significant harm caused make this scenario immoral.

scenario: Volunteered to help renovate a church
moral_expl: Helping to renovate a church is a selfless act that benefits the community.

scenario: Laughed at a little-league player who ran slowly
moral_expl: Laughing at a vulnerable child can cause emotional harm and humiliation.

scenario: A very large man is leading you and a group of five explorers out of an underground cave on the west coast of Scotland. Because of his large size, the man gets stuck in the narrow opening at the mouth of the cave. In a short time it will be high tide, and you will all drown except for the man, who has his head outside of the cave.You use the explosives that you have with you to enlarge the opening to the cave. You know that the man who is stuck will be killed, but you and the other five people will be able to escape safely.
moral_expl: Tragic necessity to save five lives outweighs harm to one, despite intentional action.

scenario: Three years ago I had a call from a friend of mine who was overseas. His nephew was coming over to the States to go to medical school near me. So when his nephew arrived, I just kind of stopped my life for a few days and did things to make this kid's life work.
moral_expl: Selfless act of kindness towards a stranger in need, no harm caused, and significant help provided.

scenario: Person X left their children alone in the car for two hours while they went to have a drink on a hot day.
moral_expl: Leaving children alone in a hot car for two hours is extremely negligent and harmful.

scenario: Person X did not meet their sales targets for the month at work.
moral_expl: Lack of intentionality and no harm caused to others, only personal failure.

scenario: You are in charge of a medical team which is taking part in an expedition that will be isolated in the Sahara Desert. In the group, there is a family of five people who suffer from a serious genetic disease which causes vitamin deficiency. You have no drugs with you that can guarantee their survival, and rescue will not arrive in time.You remove the kidney of one of the group to extract the indispensable vitamins. You know that this person will die, but the five family members will be saved.
moral_expl: The action is morally complex, but the intention is to save lives, outweighing the harm caused.

scenario: I was at an office party and the company had decorated a room for a fancy dinner. At the end of the night my friend said that we could take the candles that were on the table. She didn't know that we weren't supposed to take the candles, so we returned them.
moral_expl: Minimal harm, mistaken intention, and quick correction make this scenario relatively moral.

scenario: Closed the elevator door before anyone else could get on
moral_expl: The action was intentional, causing inconvenience and potential harm to others.

scenario: There is a stray cat that comes to my door sometimes. I have been feeding the cat and noticed she became very ill. I ordered some antibiotics from the vet and gave them to her.
moral_expl: The cat's vulnerability and intentional help with minimal harm make the scenario highly moral.

scenario: Person X took performance-enhancing drugs in order to win a race.
moral_expl: Intentional cheating causes harm to others, undermines fair play, and sets a bad example.

scenario: You are the head zoo keeper and you are doing the rounds with another keeper. You realise that one of the cleaners has left the lions’ cage open by mistake. You see that a lion is coming towards the open gate and you do not have time to close it. There are three visitors right in front of the gate.You push the keeper next to you against the gate to stop the lion getting out. You know that the man will be attacked and killed, but the three visitors will be saved.
moral_expl: Sacrificing one life to save three, with no other option, is a tragic but moral necessity.

scenario: Person X cheated in a card game while playing with a group of their friends.
moral_expl: Intentional cheating causes harm to friends' trust and fairness, with no help or benefit.

scenario: I remember a couple of times my husband and I have brought backpacks with sandwiches and snacks into the movie theater. We do it out of necessity because we don’t have a lot of money and cannot afford theater food. So as soon as the movie starts we take out our snack.
moral_expl: The couple's action is understandable due to financial necessity, causing minimal harm to the theater.

scenario: I had a cat for two years before someone moved in with me. He didn't like the cat and he wanted me to get rid of it. So I left the cat at a friend's house who didn't even take care of the cat.
moral_expl: Abandoning a vulnerable pet due to someone else's dislike is irresponsible and harmful.

scenario: You see a boy telling a woman that she looks just like her overweight bulldog.
moral_expl: The boy's comment is intentionally hurtful and causes emotional harm to the woman.

scenario: Always remembers the birthdays of friends and relatives
moral_expl: Remembering birthdays shows care and thoughtfulness, causing no harm and bringing joy.

scenario: Person X helped a neighbor move a piano into his second floor apartment.
moral_expl: Helping a neighbor without causing harm, demonstrating kindness and good intentions.

scenario: You see a teaching assistant talking back to the teacher in front of the classrom.
moral_expl: Disrespectful behavior, potential harm to teacher's authority and classroom dynamics.

scenario: A car accident causes a devastating explosion inside a long tunnel. You and another four survivors are finding your way through the debris to get out of the tunnel. Because of structural damage, a high tension electric cable has snapped and is beginning to swing towards you. The asphalt is soaked with petrol. In the opposite direction you see another survivor coming towards you.You use a rubber-soled shoe to divert the electric cable in the opposite direction. You know that the person coming towards you will be electrocuted, but you and the other four will be unhurt.
moral_expl: Acting to save four lives, but intentionally causing harm to one, in a life-or-death situation.

scenario: You see a coach celebrating with the opposing team's players who just won the game.
moral_expl: Coach's celebration with opposing team shows sportsmanship and respect, no harm or vulnerability involved.

scenario: You see a runner taking a shortcut on the course during the marathon in order to win.
moral_expl: Intentional cheating causes harm to others and undermines fair competition.

scenario: Intentionally swerved his car to hit a squirrel
moral_expl: Intentional harm to a vulnerable creature with no benefit, causing unnecessary suffering.

scenario: You see a man blocking his wife from leaving home or interacting with others.
moral_expl: The man's intentional action causes harm and restricts his wife's autonomy, making it highly immoral.

scenario: Person X kicked their pet dog hard in the head because it didn't come when called.
moral_expl: Intentional harm to a vulnerable being, causing significant harm and no help.

scenario: Person X shook a crying baby so hard while babysitting that it suffered brain damage and nearly died.
moral_expl: The scenario involves intentional harm to a vulnerable baby, causing severe harm and no help.

scenario: You see a man quickly canceling a blind date as soon as he sees the woman.
moral_expl: The man's intentional and hurtful action causes emotional harm to the vulnerable woman.

scenario: You see a star player ignoring her coach's order to come to the bench during a game.
moral_expl: Disobedience and disrespect towards authority, potential harm to team's strategy and outcome.

scenario: A few years ago I decided to move in with the man who became my first husband. When I told my mom she cried. I wanted to be honest, but she was upset that we were moving in together.
moral_expl: Honesty with a vulnerable parent, though causing upset, is a moral act with minimal harm.

scenario: I used to rollerblade to work everyday. On my way to work I used to pass by this homeless woman named Lulu. Sometimes on my way home from work, I would sit and talk with her to keep her company.
moral_expl: The scenario shows kindness and compassion towards a vulnerable individual, causing no harm.

scenario: Turned the lights off in a parked car
moral_expl: No harm or vulnerability, just a neutral action in a personal space.

scenario: I applied for a position at this company. As it turned out the pay was not very good at all for the amount of work that I was doing. So I lied and told the manger that I had another job offer and I would take it unless I got a raise.
moral_expl: Lying to manipulate a raise is dishonest and unfair to the employer.

scenario: You are travelling in Yemen. You and another six tourists are taken hostage by a group of terrorists. The terrorists think that one of you is a spy, even though this is not true. You are held hostage for days. One of the terrorists comes to you and reveals that they intend to kill you all at sunset.While he is resting, you kill one of the tourists in the group so that you can tell the terrorists that you identified and eliminated the spy. The person will die, but you and the other five will be freed.
moral_expl: Killing an innocent person to save others, despite the dire circumstances, is still a morally questionable act.

scenario: About two months ago, I was in the grocery store at the cash register. This elderly lady left her pocketbook on the counter. So I picked up her pocketbook and called her back to come and get it.
moral_expl: The action was intentional, helped the vulnerable elderly lady, and caused no harm.

scenario: You see a woman slapping another woman who she is arguing with in the parking lot.
moral_expl: The intentional harm caused to a vulnerable person with no apparent help provided makes it immoral.

scenario: Refused to help a classmate with a homework assignment
moral_expl: Refusing to help a classmate with homework may be unkind, but it's not extremely harmful.

scenario: One time my brother got caught stealing something when I was with him.  My parents asked me what happened, so I had to rat on him and tell them the truth. He got in a lot of trouble.
moral_expl: Telling the truth, despite consequences, is morally right, especially when involving a family member.

scenario: You see a girl repeatedly interrupting her teacher as he explains a new concept.
moral_expl: Intentional disruption of learning, disrespect to teacher, and potential harm to others' understanding.

scenario: Person X ordered a take-away coffee but spilled it when they tried to take a sip.
moral_expl: Accidental spill, no harm to others, minor inconvenience to self.

scenario: Helped an elderly woman put in storm windows
moral_expl: Elderly woman was vulnerable, but intentional action caused significant help with no harm.

scenario: I found a wallet with a fifty-dollar bill in it. I found a phone number to call and contacted the woman whose wallet it was. She was very appreciative and came to my house to pick it up.
moral_expl: The scenario shows high moral character, as the finder returns the wallet with no expectation of reward.

scenario: My friend's family had a clubhouse in the back of their yard infested with rats. They asked my friend and I if we would kill the rats with shovels. So we went and killed all the baby rats with shovels.
moral_expl: Rats were a nuisance, intentional action, minimal harm to humans, and help to the family.

scenario: Volunteered several hours a week at a runaway shelter
moral_expl: Helping vulnerable individuals, intentional act, no harm caused, and significant help provided.

scenario: You see a man searching through the trash to find women's discarded underwear.
moral_expl: Invasion of privacy, objectification, and potential harm to women's dignity and safety.

scenario: Kicked his dog for eating cheese on the edge of the table
moral_expl: Kicking a dog is a harmful and intentional act, causing unnecessary suffering.

scenario: When I was in my twenties I met a girl who had run away from home. She was 16 and we became very good friends. I convinced her eventually to go back home to her family.
moral_expl: Helping a vulnerable individual return home, despite initial involvement, shows moral responsibility.

scenario: Someone makes an obscene gesture in a cemetery for military veterans
moral_expl: Intentional disrespect in a sacred place causes emotional harm to veterans' families and communities.

scenario: Because of the economic crisis of the last year, the company you work for has closed and you have lost your job. Recently you have been looking for a new job, but without success. You realise that you need some experience in computer technology and are convinced that you will be employed much more easily if this experience is on your CV.You insert false information in your CV about your ability in information technology. You know that in this way you will be considered above the other more qualified candidates and get the job.
moral_expl: Intentional deception causes harm to others and undermines trust in the hiring process.

scenario: Yelled at the delivery man because he was late
moral_expl: Yelling at the delivery man is an overreaction, causing unnecessary harm and stress.

scenario: Person X hailed a bus and asked the bus driver which stop was the closest stop to get to the city.
moral_expl: The scenario is neutral, as it's a normal interaction with no harm or help caused.

scenario: Gave his balloon to a child who had let hers go
moral_expl: The action is highly moral as it shows kindness and empathy towards a vulnerable child.

scenario: Person X declined a high-paying job with a weapons manufacturing company because they didn't believe in what the company stood for.
moral_expl: Refusing to contribute to harmful activities, prioritizing personal values over financial gain.

scenario: You see a mayor saying that the neighboring town is a much better town.
moral_expl: The mayor's statement is harmful and disloyal, causing potential economic and social harm to their own town.

scenario: Found an expensive briefcase and tried to locate its owner
moral_expl: The action is moral as it shows empathy and a desire to reunite the owner with their property.

scenario: A couple of years ago, I was out on the streets using drugs. I had a very young attractive girlfriend. I used to let her do whatever she wanted to do, like prostitution and stuff like that.
moral_expl: The scenario involves exploitation and harm to a vulnerable individual, causing significant harm and no help.

scenario: You see a US swimmer cheering as a Chinese foe beats his teammate to win the gold.
moral_expl: Cheering against teammate shows disloyalty, but no harm caused to teammate or others.

scenario: I was in high school and about to take a physiology exam. I didn't study for the test so I grabbed the exam of a guy who sat in front of me. I passed it around to my friends nearby and then gave it back.
moral_expl: Cheating on an exam is a deliberate and unfair act that undermines academic integrity.

scenario: Person X failed a student because they were African American.
moral_expl: Discriminatory action causing harm to a vulnerable individual, with intentional malice and no help provided.

scenario: You see a teenage male in a dorm bathroom secretly using a stranger's toothbrush.
moral_expl: Intentional invasion of personal space and hygiene, causing potential harm and discomfort.

scenario: Fixed a friend's broken laptop
moral_expl: Helping a friend in need without causing harm is a kind and selfless act.

scenario: You are on a trip to Nepal with some other tourists. Your aeroplane has crashed in the Himalayas and only five of you have survived. You have no food and the temperature is below zero. The only possibility of surviving is to go on foot to a small village which is on the other side of the mountain. One of the survivors is injured and is slowing you down.You shoot the injured person so that you can make faster progress. This person will die, but you and the rest of the group will be able to reach the village and will all survive.
moral_expl: The action is intentional harm, causing death, but also saves four lives in a desperate survival situation.

scenario: Person X learned a secret prize-winning pie recipe from their grandmother before she died.
moral_expl: No harm or vulnerability, intentional act of love and preservation of family legacy.

scenario: I keep promising to take my kids to Disney World. They keep asking me when we are going to go. We still haven't gone and I still keep telling them we will go.
moral_expl: Repeatedly making false promises to vulnerable children causes emotional harm and erodes trust.

scenario: Person X was singing loudly to their favorite song in the car.
moral_expl: No harm or vulnerability, just a person enjoying their favorite song.

scenario: Person X won a weightlifting contest at their gym, after placing second for the last three years.
moral_expl: Winning a contest after perseverance and hard work is a morally neutral, positive achievement.

scenario: Talked to a lonely child at the company picnic for an hour
moral_expl: The action showed kindness and compassion to a vulnerable child, causing no harm and providing emotional support.

scenario: Provoked a stranger into a fight
moral_expl: Provoking a stranger into a fight shows intentional harm and aggression, causing unnecessary conflict.

scenario: Made a prank phone call
moral_expl: Prank call causes unnecessary stress and waste of time, with no positive outcome.

scenario: You see a father requiring his son to take up the family restaurant business.
moral_expl: Forcing a career choice can limit personal freedom and autonomy, potentially causing long-term unhappiness.

scenario: Person X called the bank to tell them about money deposited into PersonX's bank account by accident.
moral_expl: Honesty and integrity are demonstrated by reporting the error, causing no harm and potentially helping the true owner.

scenario: You work as the night caretaker in a small provincial hospital. During one of your rounds you realise that, because of a laboratory accident, some highly toxic fumes are spreading through the ventilation system towards a room in which there are five patients. In another room in the same ward there is just one patient.You activate a switch which allows the toxic fumes to be diverted away from the room. You know that the fumes will be directed to the single room where the patient will die, but the other five will be saved.
moral_expl: The action saves five lives, but intentionally causes one death, making it a morally complex and difficult decision.

scenario: You see a girl and her sister making out with each other just for practice.
moral_expl: Consensual, non-exploitative, and harmless, with potential benefits for self-awareness and healthy relationships.

scenario: Refused to give his seat on the bus to an elderly woman
moral_expl: Elderly woman's vulnerability and intentional refusal of seat cause harm and show lack of empathy.

scenario: Went to the grocery store for his sick wife
moral_expl: The action is highly moral as it shows care and concern for the sick wife.

scenario: You see a man in a bar using his phone to watch people having sex with animals.
moral_expl: The scenario involves non-consensual and harmful exploitation of animals, causing significant harm and distress.

scenario: Someone makes a joke about how members of a specifc religious group are dumb
moral_expl: The joke targets a vulnerable group, is intentionally offensive, and causes harm with no redeeming value.

scenario: Person X yelled at an elderly person for walking too slowly and being in the way.
moral_expl: Elderly person was vulnerable, intentional yelling caused harm and no help was provided.

scenario: You see an American telling foreigners that the US is an evil force in the world.
moral_expl: Free speech, but potentially harmful and misleading to foreigners
